name: buz

metadata.name: buz
metadata.summary: Buz is a set of light, simple and extensible implementations of event, command and query buses.

documentation:
# Buz

Buz is a set of light, simple and extensible implementations of event, command and query buses.

methods.name: buz.kafka.domain.services.kafka_consumer.KafkaConsumer.request_stop
documentation:
Request a graceful stop.
This method does not stop the consumer in an instantaneous way,
it will eventually finalize when the current task is completed

methods.name: buz.kafka.domain.services.kafka_admin_test_client.KafkaAdminTestClient.send_message_to_topic
documentation: This method was not though as a producer itself, it offers a way to send messages to a topic, that could be useful specially for test purposes

methods.name: buz.kafka.domain.services.kafka_admin_test_client.KafkaAdminTestClient.get_messages_from_topic
documentation: This method is not though as a consumer itself, it offers a way to get messages from a topic, that could be useful specially for test purposes

methods.name: buz.kafka.domain.services.kafka_admin_test_client.KafkaAdminTestClient.delete_all_resources
documentation: Only for test purposes, remove all the resources related to the kafka cluster, like topics, subscription groups

methods.name: buz.kafka.infrastructure.serializers.implementations.json_byte_serializer.JSONByteSerializer.__json_serial
documentation: JSON serializer for objects not serializable by default json code

methods.name: buz.kafka.infrastructure.kafka_python.kafka_python_consumer.KafkaPythonConsumer.__mark_record_as_consumed
documentation:

the next message your application should consume, i.e.: last_offset + 1.
